caption: Fig. 2 | Improving catalytic efficiency through low-throughput screening of FuncLib designs. a, Catalytic efficiencies of 12 FuncLib designs encoding 5–8 active-site mutations relative to Des27. Data represent mean $\pm s . d$ . of 2–5 biological replicates, except for Des27.2 and Des27.3 $( n = 1 )$ . b, Michaelis–Menten analysis of Des27.7. Data are the mean of two technical repeats. c, The crystal structure of the ligand-unbound Des27.7 (grey, PDB entry 9HVB) verifies the accuracy of the designed active site (blue) with r.m.s.d. $< 0 . 5 \mathring \mathrm { A }$ .
topics: low-throughput screening|catalytic efficiency|Michaelis–Menten kinetics|crystal structure validation|active-site mutations
description: Panel a compares catalytic efficiencies of 12 FuncLib-designed enzyme variants (5–8 active-site mutations) against Des27; panel b shows Michaelis–Menten kinetics for the best variant Des27.7; panel c presents the ligand-free crystal structure of Des27.7 (PDB 9HVB) confirming the designed active-site geometry with <0.5 Å r.m.s.d.
key_findings: FuncLib designs improve catalytic efficiency over Des27|Des27.7 exhibits superior kinetics|crystal structure validates designed active-site mutations with sub-0.5 Å accuracy

description: Panel a correlates three mutations (positions 136, 216, 236) with rising catalytic efficiency and more favorable Rosetta vdW energy of catalytic Asp162 (Spearman ρ = ‑0.88, P = 6×10⁻⁵). Panel d notes two catalytically relevant binding modes observed for the substrate in MD.

caption: competent conformations: one in which the nitro group is buried inside the TIM barrel (in, blue) and another in which it is solvent exposed (out, orange). Shown are two representative conformations from the MD simulations. e, Activation free energy for the proton abstraction of 5-nitrobenzisoxazole, comparing $\Delta G ^ { \ddagger }$ computed from the experimentally determined $k _ { \mathrm { c a t } }$ values (mean ± s.d. of 2 and 5 biological replicates for Des27 and Des27.7, respectively) using the Eyring rate equation, assuming $T = 2 9 8 \mathsf { K }$ (experiment), and the corresponding values calculated for ‘in’ and ‘out’ substrate conformations (mean ± s.d. over 30 independent EVB trajectories per system). A two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum test (two-sided) indicated statistically significant differences in the calculated activation free energies between the ‘in’ and ‘out’ conformations for Des27.7 $\mathrm { \mathit { P } } { = } 7 . 7 \times 1 0 ^ { - 9 } )$ but not for Des27 $\scriptstyle ( P = 0 . 0 8 8$ ). R.e.u., Rosetta energy units.
topics: substrate conformational sampling|activation free energy|proton abstraction|molecular dynamics|EVB calculations
description: Two dominant substrate conformations (nitro group buried ‘in’ or solvent-exposed ‘out’) identified by MD inside the TIM barrel, and their computed activation free energies for proton abstraction compared with experimental kcat-derived values for Des27 and Des27.7.
data_types: MD representative conformations|activation free energy (ΔG‡)|experimental kcat|EVB trajectories|statistical test (Wilcoxon rank-sum)
key_findings: Des27.7 shows a statistically significant difference in ΔG‡ between ‘in’ and ‘out’ conformations (P = 7.7×10⁻⁹), whereas Des27 does not (P = 0.088)|Computed ΔG‡ values for both conformations align closely with experimentally derived ΔG‡ from kcat

caption: Fig. 4 | Deconvoluting the contributions of the mutations in Des27.7 reveals necessary and sufficient components for effective de novo enzyme design. Apparent melting temperature and catalytic efficiency of Des27.7 (top row) are compared with variants in which design components are ablated (X symbols). The number of mutations relative to the modular assembly baseline is indicated in parentheses. Data represent the mean ± s.d. of 2–5 biological replicates. Muts, number of mutations; ND, not detected.
topics: de novo enzyme design|mutational analysis|thermostability|catalytic efficiency
description: Systematic removal of design mutations from the final variant Des27.7 to identify which mutations are necessary and sufficient for high melting temperature and catalytic efficiency.
key_findings: Specific subsets of mutations are required to maintain high thermostability and catalytic efficiency; ablating certain design components lowers Tm and/or kcat/Km; the figure quantifies how many mutations can be removed before activity or stability is lost.

caption: Extended Data Fig. 2 | Representative Michaelis-Menten plots. The data were fitted to the Michaelis-Menten equation $\nu _ { 0 } { = } k _ { \mathrm { c a t } } [ \mathrm { E } ] _ { 0 } [ \mathrm { S } ] _ { 0 } / ( [ \mathrm { S } ] _ { 0 } + K _ { \mathrm { M } } )$ . When substrate saturation could not be attained due to limited substrate solubility, the data were fitted to the linear region of the Michaelis-Menten model and $\mathbf { v } _ { 0 } = [ \mathsf { S } ] _ { 0 } [ \mathsf { E } ] _ { 0 } k _ { \mathrm { c a t } } / K _ { \mathrm { M } }$ , and $k _ { \mathrm { { c a t } } } / K _ { \mathrm { { M } } }$ were deduced from the slope. Kinetic experiments were performed for all functional designs in this study with at least two technical replicates, and high-functioning designs were further evaluated with 2–5 biological replicates. Data presented are mean of 2 technical repeats.
topics: kinetic analysis|Michaelis-Menten kinetics|substrate solubility limitation|enzyme characterization
description: The figure displays representative Michaelis-Menten plots for enzyme designs, showing how initial velocity varies with substrate concentration; when saturation could not be reached due to low substrate solubility, only the linear region was used to extract kcat/KM.

caption: Extended Data Fig. 5 | Time evolution of the active site center of mass (COM) and ligand distance during total 5 × 1 μs of MD simulations. a. Des27 and b. Des27.7. The dotted gray line at 4 Å marks the threshold for defining the ligand as being within the active site. In two out of ten replicas across the two systems, we observe ligand dissociation without rebinding towards the end of the trajectory on the timescale of our simulations; in a third, in the case of Des27.7, we observe substantial dissociation mid-trajectory with ligand rebinding within the simulation timeframes.
description: 5 × 1 µs MD replicas track the distance between the ligand and the active-site center-of-mass; a 4 Å cutoff defines bound vs unbound states for two enzyme variants.
key_findings: Ligand remains bound within 4 Å in the majority of replicas|Two replicas show irreversible ligand dissociation|One Des27.7 replica shows transient dissociation followed by rebinding

caption: Extended Data Fig. 8 | EVB equilibration dynamics and representative structures of the Des27 system. a-b Root mean square deviations (rmsd, Å) of all solute atoms of the Des27 and Des27.7 calculated for the equilibration phase prior to our EVB simulations. Data were collected every 10 ps from the initial equilibration runs and shown as averages and standard deviations over ten individual 25 ns MD simulations per system (i.e., 750 ns cumulative simulation time per system). The average rmsd per system is denoted by the colored solid line, and the standard deviations per point over all trajectories are illustrated by the shaded area on each plot. a. rmsd for EVB equilibration from “out” substrate conformation. b. rmsd for EVB equilibration from “in” substrate conformation. c-d EVB Representative structures of the Des27 system. c. For the “in” ligand conformation. d. For the “out” ligand conformation. Michaelis complex (MC, left panel), transition state (TS, middle panel), and product complex (PC, right panel) for the KE reaction catalyzed by this enzyme, extracted from EVB trajectories of this reaction. Structures were selected based on clustering analysis. The clustering was performed at the MC, TS and PC independently, in order to obtain representative structures for each state. Donor-acceptor distances (Å) are shown for each stationary point. These values are averages of the snapshots taken every 5 ps of the trajectory, determined based on the combined evaluation of 30 replicas.
topics: EVB simulation equilibration|substrate conformation sampling|representative catalytic structures|donor-acceptor distances
description: Equilibration RMSD traces for Des27 and Des27.7 prior to EVB simulations starting from 'in' and 'out' substrate poses, plus clustered representative structures (Michaelis, TS, product) for Des27 with average donor-acceptor distances extracted from 30 EVB replicas.
data_types: RMSD time series|EVB clustering|donor-acceptor distance averages|representative catalytic state structures
key_findings: Both variants reach stable RMSD within 25 ns equilibration|Substrate 'in' and 'out' conformations were both sampled|Clustered MC, TS and PC structures show consistent donor-acceptor distances across 30 replicas
